title: Eval gates: provenance + caveat coverage + suppression + content-safety lint (CI)
context: With the statistics-correctness layer (incidence-explorers-stats-009) and accessible dashboards (incidence-explorers-dashboard-010) built, the project needs a comprehensive eval/quality harness in CI to enforce all safety, provenance, caveat, suppression, and content-safety rules as automated gates. A failing gate must fail the build. This harness protects against regressions and enables M3 patient-facing content to be built on top of a reliably clean foundation.
objective: Implement the eval/quality harness (scripts/eval.ts) as CI gates: (1) provenance coverage — 100% of rendered statistics resolve to a Source; (2) caveat coverage — 100% of figures show the mandatory caveats from the per-figure caveat matrix; (3) suppression safety — 0 suppressed or <16-case values reach the render layer; (4) content-safety lint — 0 advice/diagnosis/prognosis phrasings in any content. All four gates must fail CI when violated.
acceptanceCriteria: Provenance-coverage gate: 100% of rendered statistics carry a resolvable Source; a statistic without a source fails CI

Caveat-coverage gate: 100% of figures carry the required caveats per the caveat matrix (counts-vs-rates, age-standardisation + standard pop, CI/estimate uncertainty, screening artifact where relevant, registry lag); missing caveats fail CI
Suppression-safety gate: 0 suppressed or <16-case values can reach the rendering layer; any suppressed value passing through fails CI
Content-safety lint gate: 0 advice/diagnosis/prognosis/individual-risk phrasings in any content; any such phrasing fails CI
All four gates are wired into CI and fail the build on violation; a failing gate cannot be bypassed silently
CI is green (build/test/lint); reviewed and signed off by the Data-governance reviewer and Statistics reviewer
output: A merged PR implementing the four-gate eval/quality harness (provenance coverage, caveat coverage, suppression safety, content-safety lint) as CI gates that fail the build on violation — the quality enforcement layer required before M3 patient-facing content investment.
